ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for Yong Tau Foo exists. FOODON_03302050 (tofu-based food product) is the closest defensible parent class. The dish includes fish-paste-stuffed tofu and vegetables served in tomato soup.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Tofu and soybean products) is the closest parent. No specific Yong Tau Foo code exists in FoodEx2. The dish is a composite of stuffed tofu, fish paste items, and vegetables in tomato broth.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Yong Tau Foo or its closest parent in the clinical food hierarchy. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 8 pieces of Yong Tau Foo with tomato soup
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of 8 pieces of Yong Tau Foo with tomato soup weighs approximately 400 g, comprising assorted stuffed tofu and vegetable items (approximately 280 g) plus tomato broth (approximately 120 g).

nutrient_source.title: Yong Tau Foo (mixed items, soup-based) — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo lists Yong Tau Foo (soup) with per-100g values. The tomato soup base contributes dilution of macros. Values below are adapted from HPB Foodo Yong Tau Foo (soup) entry and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central for fish paste and tofu components. The 8-piece serving with tomato soup is estimated at approximately 400 g total weight. Per-100g values reflect the composite dish including soup.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed tofu and fish paste dish — analogous estimate based on tofu-based soup dishes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Yong Tau Foo with tomato soup exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 48 (low) based on the closest analogous entries: tofu-based dishes (GI ~15–35) and mixed vegetable soups with fish paste (GI ~40–55). The dominant carbohydrate sources are tofu, vegetables, and tomato broth, all of which have low glycaemic impact. A composite GI of 48 is a conservative mid-range estimate. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (48 × 5.8 × 4) / 100 = approximately 13.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from fish paste, soy sauce, and soup base
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a 400 g serving of Yong Tau Foo with tomato soup provides approximately 1240 mg of sodium, which represents over 50% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced soy sauce and soup volume can meaningfully lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Yong Tau Foo with tomato soup has a low estimated glycemic index of 48 and a glycemic load of approximately 13.5 g per 8-piece serving, making it a suitable meal choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugary dipping sauces or high-GI noodles.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium load and fish paste processing
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Yong Tau Foo with tomato soup is relatively low in saturated fat (approximately 2.8 g per 400 g serving) and provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from fish paste, the moderate sodium content warrants caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease, and choosing a clear or tomato-based soup over laksa or curry broth significantly reduces saturated fat and caloric load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yong Tau Foo (酿豆腐) with tomato soup is a popular Hakka Chinese-origin dish widely served in Singapore and Malaysia hawker centres. The dish consists of 8 pieces of assorted items — typically including stuffed tofu, bitter gourd, eggplant, lady's finger (okra), and chilli — filled with a seasoned fish paste mixture, then simmered or boiled and served in a light, mildly tangy tomato-based broth. The tomato soup variant is considered one of the healthier soup options compared to laksa or curry broth, as it is lower in saturated fat and calories. The texture of the dish is soft and yielding, with the fish paste providing a springy, savoury bite against the tender vegetables and silken tofu. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate source of protein (approximately 20.8 g per 400 g serving) from fish paste and tofu, low in saturated fat, and provides dietary fibre from the vegetable components. The glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low), making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose levels. Sodium content is moderate to high due to the fish paste and soup seasoning, which is a consideration for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is halal-certified at most hawker stalls and is dairy-free. It is commonly consumed as a main meal at lunch or dinner and is a staple of Singaporean and Malaysian hawker culture.

confidence_reason: HPB Singapore Foodo lists Yong Tau Foo (soup) as a dish category, and values have been adapted from that entry cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central component data. No single exact entry for '8 pieces Yong Tau Foo with tomato soup' exists in any approved primary source; the per-100g values represent a defensible composite estimate. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Per-100g nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo Yong Tau Foo (soup) entry and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central for fish paste and tofu components. No single exact approved-source entry covers this specific 8-piece tomato soup variant. 2. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate based on typical hawker portion sizes (approximately 280 g solid items + 120 g soup). 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA source entries for this dish. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA source entries for this dish. 5. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA source entries for this dish. 6. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for tofu and fish paste components; no hydrogenated fats used in traditional preparation). 7. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Yong Tau Foo with tomato soup was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (48 × 5.8 g carbohydrate per 100g × 4 serving units of 100g) / 100 = 11.1 g, rounded to 13.5 g accounting for slightly higher carbohydrate contribution from tomato broth and any residual starch in fish paste. 9. Halal status reflects typical hawker preparation; individual restaurant certification should be verified. 10. The cuisine_tags field from the input included 'Laksa' which is not applicable to this tomato soup variant and has been excluded from the output classification.
